title: How a Pusher Centrifuge Works
snippet: Pusher centrifuges possess filtration bowls with metal screening sheets or slotted sieves; an oscillating pushing motion transports the filter cakes out of the bowls.

title: How a Self Cleaning Decanter Centrifuge Works
snippet: Self-cleaning decanter centrifuges separate solids from liquids efficiently (published April 16, 2024).

title: How a Basket Centrifuge Works
snippet: Feed slurry is introduced into the rotating basket and accelerated to basket speed, with solids pulled radially away from the liquid and collecting along the inner wall.

title: How Does a Decanter Centrifuge Work? by Centrifuge.com
snippet: Solid particles, heavier than liquid, migrate outwards in the revolving bowl due to centrifugal force, forming sediment on the inside of the bowl's inner wall.

title: How a Nozzle Separator Centrifuge Works
snippet: Nozzle separators are continuously operating disk centrifuges built as centrifugal clarifiers and separators; solids discharged continuously through nozzles at the bowl periphery.

title: How a Peeler Centrifuge Works
snippet: Peeler centrifuges are batch centrifuges with perforated filtration bowls; solids form on the cloth filter and are scraped out by peeler knives.
